scoring_note: This rubric evaluates whether content is structured for AI retrieval, understanding, summarization, and citation-readiness. It is not a traditional SEO ranking rubric and does not guarantee AI citation.

dimensions.name: Query-answer coverage
dimensions.description: Measures whether the page directly addresses the target questions and commercially relevant query variants.
dimensions.what_good_looks_like: The page includes clear, complete answers to the target questions using language close to how the audience asks them.
dimensions.what_bad_looks_like: The page talks around the topic but does not directly answer the target questions or leaves important query intents uncovered.
dimensions.scoring_guidance: Score higher when target question terms, intents, and answer passages are clearly present. Score lower when answers are missing, vague, or spread across unrelated sections.
evidence_to_collect: Question terms found in page text
Question terms missing from page text
dimensions.recommended_fix_logic: For each missing or weakly covered question, add a concise answer block that mirrors the user's wording and then expands with useful detail.

dimensions.name: Direct answer extractability
dimensions.description: Measures whether AI systems can extract a clear answer passage without inferring from scattered copy.
dimensions.what_good_looks_like: Key sections start with a direct, declarative answer before supporting explanation, examples, or nuance.
dimensions.what_bad_looks_like: Answers are buried, implied, overly promotional, or dependent on long context to understand.
dimensions.scoring_guidance: Score higher when the page uses BLUF-style answers, short answer paragraphs, and declarative statements. Score lower when answer candidates are long, vague, or fragmented.
dimensions.recommended_fix_logic: Rewrite important sections so the first one or two sentences answer the question directly before adding context.

dimensions.name: Heading and content structure
dimensions.description: Measures whether the page is organized into readable, retrievable content blocks with useful headings.
dimensions.what_good_looks_like: The page uses descriptive H1/H2/H3 headings, short sections, logical order, and content blocks that can stand alone.
dimensions.what_bad_looks_like: The page has weak headings, long undifferentiated copy, unclear section flow, or content that is difficult to chunk.
dimensions.scoring_guidance: Score higher when headings describe answers and content is block-structured for retrieval. Score lower when headings are generic or the page is hard to parse.
dimensions.recommended_fix_logic: Add descriptive headings and break long sections into focused answer blocks with one clear idea per section.

dimensions.name: FAQ readiness
dimensions.description: Measures whether the page includes question-and-answer structures that map to natural AI retrieval patterns.
dimensions.what_good_looks_like: The page includes relevant FAQs or question-style headings with concise, direct answers.
dimensions.what_bad_looks_like: The page has no FAQ structure, misses obvious long-tail questions, or provides answers that are too vague to retrieve.
dimensions.scoring_guidance: Score higher when high-priority questions appear as headings or FAQs with direct answers. Score lower when question formats are absent or incomplete.
evidence_to_collect: Target questions answered in FAQ format
dimensions.recommended_fix_logic: Add an FAQ section that answers priority questions in plain language, using each question as a heading.

dimensions.name: Entity clarity
dimensions.description: Measures whether key entities are explicitly named and easy for AI systems to identify.
dimensions.what_good_looks_like: The page clearly identifies the brand, offer, audience, market, topic, people, places, and other required entities.
dimensions.what_bad_looks_like: Important entities are missing, inconsistently named, or only implied through vague wording.
dimensions.scoring_guidance: Score higher when required entities appear naturally in titles, headings, body copy, and metadata. Score lower when entity mentions are sparse or ambiguous.
dimensions.recommended_fix_logic: Add missing required entities in natural language across headings, introductory copy, and relevant answer passages.

dimensions.name: Semantic relationship clarity
dimensions.description: Measures whether the page explains how entities, topics, benefits, proof points, and actions relate to one another.
dimensions.what_good_looks_like: The content clearly connects who the brand helps, what it offers, why it matters, how it works, and what outcomes it supports.
dimensions.what_bad_looks_like: The page lists concepts without explaining relationships, causality, use cases, comparisons, or outcomes.
dimensions.scoring_guidance: Score higher when the page uses relationship language such as helps, includes, because, for example, compared with, supports, and results in. Score lower when relationships are implicit.
dimensions.recommended_fix_logic: Add sentences that explicitly connect entities and concepts, such as who uses the offer, what problem it solves, and why it matters.

dimensions.name: Evidence and source support
dimensions.description: Measures whether claims are grounded with proof points that improve citation-readiness.
dimensions.what_good_looks_like: The page supports claims with examples, data, case studies, testimonials, source links, methodology, or third-party validation.
dimensions.what_bad_looks_like: The page makes broad claims without evidence, examples, sources, or validation.
dimensions.scoring_guidance: Score higher when important claims have concrete support. Score lower when claims are promotional, unsupported, or impossible to verify from the page.
dimensions.recommended_fix_logic: Add proof for major claims using examples, source links, data points, third-party validation, or short case evidence.

dimensions.name: Schema readiness
dimensions.description: Measures whether the page includes structured data signals that help machines understand page type and entities.
dimensions.what_good_looks_like: Relevant schema such as Organization, Article, FAQPage, Person, Service, or BreadcrumbList is present and aligned with visible content.
dimensions.what_bad_looks_like: Schema is missing, incomplete, inaccurate, or disconnected from the visible page content.
dimensions.scoring_guidance: Score higher when relevant schema types are present and consistent with the page. Score lower when schema is absent or mismatched.
dimensions.recommended_fix_logic: Add or improve schema that reflects the visible page content and key entities.

dimensions.name: Brand-topic association
dimensions.description: Measures whether the page clearly associates the brand with the target topic and commercial category.
dimensions.what_good_looks_like: The brand is repeatedly and naturally connected to the target topic, offer, audience, and market.
dimensions.what_bad_looks_like: The brand and topic appear separately, rarely, or without a clear relationship.
dimensions.scoring_guidance: Score higher when brand-topic pairings appear in titles, headings, answer passages, metadata, and supporting copy. Score lower when the connection is weak or implied.
dimensions.recommended_fix_logic: Add natural strategic repetition that ties the brand to the topic, offer, audience, and market.

dimensions.name: Conversion alignment
dimensions.description: Measures whether the page gives a clear next step that matches the user's likely intent after reading an AI-generated answer.
dimensions.what_good_looks_like: The page includes a relevant, low-friction next action such as booking, contacting, requesting an audit, downloading a resource, or reading a related page.
dimensions.what_bad_looks_like: The page answers questions but does not guide the reader toward a useful next step.
dimensions.scoring_guidance: Score higher when next actions are clear, relevant, and aligned with the content intent. Score lower when calls to action are missing, generic, or disconnected.
dimensions.recommended_fix_logic: Add a clear next action that fits the page intent and helps the reader move from answer consumption to action.
